title: Esters
summary: 9701 AS ester formation and hydrolysis — naming, equilibrium esterification, and saponification with PhET molecule builder.
learningObjectives: Name esters using alkyl alkanoate convention.
Relate ester structures to uses in flavours and biodiesel.

simpleExplanation.title: Ester linkage –COO–
simpleExplanation.summary: Esters form from carboxylic acids and alcohols. The reaction is reversible — remove water to shift equilibrium to products.
steps: R–COOH + R′OH ⇌ R–COOR′ + H₂O (conc. H₂SO₄, heat).
Naming: alkyl from alcohol + alkanoate from acid chain.

sections.content: **Esters** (18.2) are tested with **mechanisms** and **identification** by smell or hydrolysis products. Link to **carboxylic acids** (18.1) and **amides** (A Level).
